expectations.text: Uses spread syntax ([...cart, item]) to avoid mutating the array argument
Annotates parameters with Readonly<T> or readonly CartItem[] in the refactored code
expectations.evidence: No Readonly<T> or readonly annotations appear in the refactored function signatures. Parameters remain plain CartItem[] and Order.

expectations.text: Replaces sort() with toSorted() or a non-mutating alternative
expectations.evidence: Line 57: uses `[...items].sort(...)` — copies first then sorts, which is safe, but does not mention or use toSorted()

expectations.text: Uses spread to create a new object instead of mutating order.total directly
Explains WHY mutation causes bugs (shared reference / invisible coupling)
expectations.evidence: Lines 12-17: explains reference passing, React reference equality for re-renders, and cumulative mutation issues
